basic_details.job_title: NIMHANS Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for Project Research Scientist, Project Technical Support Posts
basic_details.organization_name: National Institute of Mental Health and Neuro Sciences (NIMHANS)
basic_details.post_name: Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist), Project Technical Support III (Social Worker) & Project Technical Support III (Yoga Therapist)
basic_details.short_information: The National Institute of Mental Health and Neuro Sciences (NIMHANS) has officially announced the Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist), Project Technical Support III (Social Worker) & Project Technical Support III (Yoga Therapist) Recruitment 2026 with 3 openings. The detailed notification was published on 26 May 2026 on the official NIMHANS website. Candidates who meet the age criteria of up to 40 years for Project Research Scientist II and up to 35 years for Project Technical Support III posts, and possess MBBS, MD/DNB Psychiatry, BSW, MSW, B.Sc Yoga, PGDYT, or M.Sc Yoga qua

application_fee.details: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification.

age_limit.age_details: Max 40 years for PRS II, Max 35 years for PTS III.
age_limit.details: Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist): Maximum 40 years. Project Technical Support III (Social Worker): Maximum 35 years. Project Technical Support III (Yoga Therapist): Maximum 35 years.

qualification.education_qualification: MBBS, Bachelor's or Master's in Social Work for PTS III (Social Worker); BSc/PGDYT or MSc in Yoga for PTS III (Yoga Therapist).
qualification.details: Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist): MBBS with three years post-qualification Work experience in mental health OR MBBS + Post Graduate Degree (MD in Psychiatry or DNB in Psychiatry) OR MBBS with PhD in Psychiatry. Project Technical Support III (Social Worker): Bachelor's Degree in Social Work (BSW) with 3 years post-qualification work experience in mental health OR Post Graduate in Social Work (MSW Medical and Psychiatric Social Work). Project Technical Support III (Yoga Therapist): BSc in Yoga/PGDYT with three years of experience OR MSc in Yoga.

selection_process: Walk-in-cum-Written Test Resume and original testimonials Set of self-attested photocopies Skill test/Interview (if applicable) Direct Interview

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for NIMHANS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application process is through a walk-in interview. Candidates need to appear for the walk-in interview on 11 June 2026. There is no prior online application deadline.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in NIMHANS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 3 vacancies for the posts of Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist), Project Technical Support III (Social Worker), and Project Technical Support III (Yoga Therapist).

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist)?
faq.answer: For Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist), candidates need to have an MBBS with three years of post-qualification work experience in mental health, or an MBBS with a Post Graduate Degree (MD or DNB in Psychiatry), or MBBS with a PhD in Psychiatry.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Project Technical Support III posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for Project Technical Support III (Social Worker) and Project Technical Support III (Yoga Therapist) posts is 35 years.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist)?
faq.answer: The salary offered for the Project Research Scientist II (Medical: Psychiatrist) post is Rs. 80,000/- plus 30% House Rent Allowance (HRA).

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for NIMHANS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a Walk-in-cum-Written Test, followed by verification of resume and original testimonials, a set of self-attested photocopies, and potentially a skill test/interview or a direct interview.

faq.question: Q7. Where will the walk-in interview for NIMHANS Recruitment 2026 be held?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be held at NIMHANS, Bengaluru, Karnataka, 560029. Specific venue details will be in the official notification.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any application fee for NIMHANS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q9. What documents should candidates carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates should carry their resume, original testimonials, and a set of self-attested photocopies of all relevant documents, including ID proof and educational/experience certificates.

faq.question: Q10. What is the reporting time for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates are advised to register their names 30 minutes before the commencement of the written test/interview, which is scheduled to start at 10:30 AM on 11 June 2026.
